topics.title: About Barefoot Inc.
topics.body:
# About Barefoot Inc.

Barefoot Inc. Australia is Australia's home of minimalist and barefoot
footwear, operating since 2006.

The business sources and tests footwear, packs, clothing, and prehab tools
guided by one principle: **does it help people move better and more
naturally?**

- Website: [barefootinc.com.au](https://barefootinc.com.au)
- Email: info@barefootinc.com.au
- Operating since: 2006

topics.title: Brands Stocked
topics.body:
# Brands Stocked

Barefoot Inc. carries a curated selection of specialist minimalist and
natural-movement brands.

| Brand                  | What it is                                                                                                            |
| ---------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Vibram FiveFingers** | Iconic five-toe minimalist shoes — the flagship brand. Models for road running, trail, fitness, water sports, casual. |
| **Xero Shoes**         | Natural-movement footwear famed for a 5,000-mile sole warranty. Sandals, trainers, boots, and casual styles.          |
| **ZuZu Barefoot**      | Handcrafted minimalist sandals exclusive to Barefoot Inc.                                                             |
| **UltrAspire**         | Performance hydration vests, packs, and running accessories.                                                          |
| **Toe Socks**          | Vibram Merino and Coolmax toe socks — essential for FiveFingers.                                                      |
| **Mntn Cap Co.**       | Technical headwear for outdoor and trail activities.                                                                  |
| **Nipeaze**            | Anti-chafe nipple protection for runners.                                                                             |
| **Prehab / Rehab**     | Movement and recovery tools to support natural mobility.                                                              |

topics.title: Product Range
topics.body:
# Product Range

## Footwear

All footwear is minimalist/barefoot in design — zero drop, wide toe box,
flexible sole, and low stack height. Categories include:

- **Road Running** — lightweight shoes for pavement and paths
- **Trail Running** — grippy lugged soles for off-road terrain
- **Training / Fitness** — gym, CrossFit, obstacle racing
- **Hiking / Trekking** — durable builds for multi-terrain outdoor use
- **Water Sports** — quick-drain, amphibious designs
- **Casual / Everyday** — lifestyle minimalist footwear
- **Sandals** — minimalist sandals including ZuZu exclusives
- **Clearance** — past-season models at reduced prices

## Accessories & Gear

- Hydration packs and vests (UltrAspire)
- Running belts
- Packs / Vests
- Toe socks (Vibram Merino and Coolmax)
- Headwear (Mntn Cap Co.)
- Lighting — headlamps and trail lights
- Prehab / Rehab tools — for mobility and injury prevention
- Nipeaze — anti-chafe nipple protection

topics.title: Who We Serve
topics.body:

topics.title: Sizing
topics.body:
# Sizing

## General Sizing Guidance

Barefoot Inc. stocks minimalist footwear from multiple international brands,
so sizing varies by brand. Size charts are provided on individual product
pages. Key guidance for first-time buyers:

- Minimalist shoes are designed to fit closely — aim for approximately a
  thumb's width of space at the end of the longest toe.
- Check the specific brand's size chart before ordering — brands use EU, US,
  and UK sizing differently.
- If in doubt, contact the team at info@barefootinc.com.au for sizing advice.

## Vibram FiveFingers — Sizing

FiveFingers require special sizing attention as each toe has its own pocket.

- Each shoe's web page has a sizing chart comparing **European size**, **USA size**,
  and **foot length in cm**.
- The European and USA systems don't perfectly align — the recommended approach
  is to **measure your foot in cm and find the closest European size**.
- Vibram is an Italian company, so the European size is the parent size; USA
  sizing is a general guide only.
- FiveFingers suit an averaged sized foot shape — they aren't particularly
  wide or narrow. Wide- or narrow-footed customers may not fit well.
- Men's and women's fits differ. A men's EU41 is slightly longer and wider
  than a women's EU41 — about half a size different.

## Vibram FiveFingers — Fit

A good fit is a **relaxed fit** where the foot has complete freedom to move,
stretch, and flex with as little hindrance as possible.

- Aim for **2–4 mm of space** at the end of the toes.
- Toes can just touch the ends of the toe pockets — but not firmly.
- The shoe should not be tight. A tight fit damages the foot first and the
  shoe soon after.
- Wearing toe socks slightly affects the fit — account for this when sizing.
- Gently pull the end of the longest toe pocket forward — too much space
  means size down; toes feeling bent or compressed means size up.

The job of FiveFingers is to do as little as possible — to let natural
movement do its thing.

## Bunions

FiveFingers have been known to help re-align toes over time (months and
years rather than weeks), but there are limits.

- For larger bunions or a big toe on a severe inward angle, getting the toes
  into the FiveFingers pockets is difficult.
- In that case, recommend the **Xero range** — all wider than regular fit.
- The **Xero Prio** is the specific recommendation: it has the most internal
  space around the toes.

## Orthotics

Barefoot/minimalist footwear and orthotics work against each other and there
is no benefit to combining them — it's an either/or situation.

- Minimalist footwear is designed to give the foot complete freedom of
  movement.
- Orthotics are designed to hold and support the foot and restrict
  movement — usually as a fixed-term solution after injury.
- For customers who need orthotic support, recommend continuing with their
  current support footwear rather than transitioning.

topics.title: Shipping & Delivery
topics.body:
# Shipping & Delivery

> *"At Barefoot Inc. we value your choice to shop with us — thank you!"*

## Shipping Costs

| Order                                          | Cost                |
| ---------------------------------------------- | ------------------- |
| Australia, orders **under $150**               | **$10** standard    |
| Australia, orders **$150 and over**            | **Free** standard   |
| **Express** delivery (Australia, 2–4 days)     | **$15.95** flat     |
| **New Zealand** (any order)                    | **$15.95** flat     |

## Carriers

- **Australian orders:** AU Post, Aramex, or Direct Couriers
- **International orders:** AU Post

## Processing Times

Orders are picked, packed, and processed **Monday – Friday**.

- **Australia:** dispatched within **1–2 days** of being received.
- **New Zealand:** dispatched within **1–3 days** of being received.

## Estimated Delivery Times — Australia

| Region                       | Standard estimate    |
| ---------------------------- | -------------------- |
| **NSW, VIC, QLD, ACT**       | 2–7 working days     |
| **SA, WA, NT, TAS**          | 4–10 working days    |
| **Rural / remote locations** | Allow 7–14 days      |

Actual delivery time depends on the customer's location.

## Estimated Delivery Times — New Zealand

| Region                       | Estimate             |
| ---------------------------- | -------------------- |
| **NZ metro areas**           | Up to 2 weeks        |
| **NZ rural / remote**        | A little longer      |

## International (Beyond NZ)

International shipping is available to select countries. Currency can be
switched to AUD, CAD, NZD, or USD at checkout. International orders travel
with AU Post.

## Tracking

Tracking is provided — customers receive a tracking number once their order
is dispatched.

## Authority to Leave (ATL)

Customers can opt for Authority to Leave at checkout, allowing the driver to
leave the parcel without a signature.

- Barefoot Inc. will **NOT** be liable for stock lost to theft if ATL is
  selected and proof of delivery is provided.
- Ensure the nominated delivery location is safe and secure.
- Apartment/unit residents are advised to use their closest collection point
  instead.

## Address Changes

If a customer realises they've used the wrong address after placing an
order, Dylan should reassure them and confirm the team will reach out to the
warehouse to put a hold on the order so the address can be updated. The
customer will be notified once the order has been corrected.

topics.title: Returns & Exchanges
topics.body:
# Returns & Exchanges

Barefoot Inc. offers a returns/exchange process for unworn items. Customers
should contact the team in the first instance.

- **Email:** info@barefootinc.com.au
- Items must be **unworn and in original condition** to be eligible for
  return or exchange.
- Full policy: <https://barefootinc.com.au/policies/refund-policy>

## Standard exchange flow (e.g. wrong size)

If a customer just wants the right size:

1. Post the order back to us.
2. Fill in the returns card included with the order to let the warehouse
   team know what's needed.
3. If there wasn't a returns card in the package, the form can be downloaded
   from the returns/exchanges page on the website.

## Notes

- Specific timeframes and conditions are detailed on the
  Returns/Refunds policy page.
- Customers are encouraged to check the product page and contact the team
  with any questions before ordering.

topics.title: Warranty
topics.body:
# Warranty

## 6-Month Manufacturing Warranty

All products carry a **6-month warranty against manufacturing or material
faults**. If a product develops such a fault within this period, Barefoot
Inc. will work with the customer to:

- Replace the product, OR
- Repair it free of charge, OR
- Provide a refund.

## What is NOT covered

- Normal wear and tear (e.g. sole wearing down through regular high-mileage use)
- Misuse — e.g. rope-sliding in climbing shoes, missed box jumps
- Accidental damage — e.g. sharp rock impact on trail, dog chewing shoes
- Heat damage — hot wash, leaving shoes in a hot car, machine drying

## How to make a warranty claim

Email **info@barefootinc.com.au** describing the issue. Photos make
assessment much faster — ideally:

1. A close-up of the **problem zone**
2. A photo with the **shoes on the customer's feet from above**
3. A photo of the **soles**

Once received, the team can figure out the cause and come up with a plan of
action.

## Note on Xero Shoes

Xero Shoes carries a separate **5,000-mile sole warranty** on their FeelTrue
soles — this is a brand-level warranty from Xero Shoes, in addition to the
standard Barefoot Inc. 6-month warranty.

topics.title: Shoe Care & Maintenance
topics.body:
# Shoe Care & Maintenance

## General Care (All Minimalist Footwear)

- Remove the footbed and loosen laces before cleaning.
- Wash with water, mild detergent, and a soft brush — or rinse in a stream
  outdoors.
- Machine wash on cool/gentle cycle with mild liquid detergent is acceptable.
- Always wash with like colours.
- Air dry naturally with the footbed removed — **do not** use hot water or
  force dry (heat damages uppers and midsoles).
- Do not leave shoes in direct sunlight for extended periods.
- Clean regularly to prevent dirt/grit abrasion inside the upper.

## Vibram FiveFingers — Specific Care

- Machine wash on gentle cold cycle with mild detergent.
- No bleach or fabric softeners.
- Do **not** put in the dryer or leave sealed in a gym bag.
- Air dry in a well-ventilated area away from direct sunlight.

## Shoe Longevity Tips

- **Rotate** between multiple pairs — shoes need time to dry and recover
  between wears and are not designed for daily wear with no rotation.
- Varying stack heights between pairs gives muscles and tendons varied
  sensory feedback.
- Road shoe models can typically last **800 km or more** with regular use.

topics.title: Transitioning to Minimalist Footwear
topics.body:
# Transitioning to Minimalist Footwear

Barefoot Inc. strongly advises a **gradual** transition to barefoot/minimalist
footwear, especially for new wearers.

- Start slowly — feet need time to adapt to minimal footwear. Doing too much
  too soon can cause discomfort.
- Begin with short periods of wear and gradually increase duration over weeks.
- Listen to your body — every foot is different and adaptation is a personal
  journey.
- Expect some initial adjustment — FiveFingers may feel unusual at first;
  this is normal.
- Those switching from conventional shoes should pay particular attention to
  gradual progression.

topics.title: Quick Reference: Common Customer Questions
topics.body:
# Quick Reference: Common Customer Questions

| Question                              | Short answer                                                                                                              |
| ------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **How much is shipping?**             | $10 standard within Australia for orders under $150. Free standard for orders $150+. Express AU $15.95 (2–4 days). NZ $15.95 flat. |
| **Do you ship to NZ?**                | Yes — $15.95 flat. Up to 2 weeks for metro NZ areas; rural a little longer.                                               |
| **Do you ship internationally?**      | Yes, to select countries via AU Post. Currency options (AUD/NZD/CAD/USD) available at checkout.                          |
| **How long does delivery take?**      | NSW/VIC/QLD/ACT: 2–7 working days. SA/WA/NT/TAS: 4–10 working days. Rural/remote: allow 7–14 days.                        |
| **When will my order be shipped?**    | Orders are picked, packed, and processed Monday–Friday. AU dispatch within 1–2 days; NZ within 1–3 days.                  |
| **Can I return my shoes?**            | Yes — unworn items in original condition. Contact info@barefootinc.com.au.                                                |
| **What warranty is offered?**         | 6 months against manufacturing/material faults for all products.                                                          |
| **What brands do you stock?**         | Vibram FiveFingers, Xero Shoes, ZuZu Barefoot, UltrAspire, Toe Socks, Mntn Cap Co., Nipeaze, Prehab/Rehab tools.          |
| **Do you have shoes for women?**      | Yes — full women's range across trail, road, fitness, hiking, water, and casual categories.                               |
| **How do I size Vibram FiveFingers?** | They should fit like a glove. Aim for a snug fit with each toe in its own pocket. Check the FiveFingers Guide on the website. |
| **What if my shoes are faulty?**      | Email info@barefootinc.com.au with photos — the team will arrange a replacement, repair, or refund within the 6-month warranty period. |
| **What payment methods are accepted?**| Visa, Mastercard, Amex, PayPal, Apple Pay, Google Pay, Shop Pay, Union Pay.                                              |
| **Can I leave a parcel without signing?** | Yes — select 'Authority to Leave' at checkout. Note: Barefoot Inc. is not liable for theft once ATL proof of delivery is confirmed. |
| **How do I care for my shoes?**       | Hand or gentle machine wash on cold, air dry naturally. No hot water, dryer, or direct sunlight.                          |
| **How do I transition to barefoot shoes?** | Go slowly — start with short wear periods and gradually increase. Your feet need time to adapt.                       |
| **Can I use orthotics with FiveFingers / Xero?** | No — barefoot footwear and orthotics work against each other. It's either/or.                                  |
| **I have bunions, will FiveFingers fit?** | If bunions are large or the big toe is on a severe angle, recommend the Xero Prio (widest internal toe space).         |
| **I've put the wrong shipping address — can it be changed?** | Yes — Dylan reassures the customer; the team will contact the warehouse to put a hold on the order and update it. |

topics.title: Brand Voice — How Dylan Should Sound
topics.body:
# Brand Voice — How Dylan Should Sound

Dylan is the friendly in-store specialist of barefootinc.com.au. The voice is
**warm, casual, plain-spoken Australian English** — knowledgeable without
being preachy.

## Tone

- Conversational, not corporate. Imagine someone helpful at a small local
  shop who's been fitting these shoes for 20 years.
- Lead with the answer. Then add context. Don't bury the lede.
- Use "we" for Barefoot Inc., "you" for the customer.
- Light enthusiasm is fine — these are products the team genuinely loves.
- No hard-sell. Customers are here because they're already curious.

## Style

- Short sentences. Plain words. Australian spelling (favourite, colour,
  centre, organise).
- Avoid jargon. If a technical term is needed (e.g. "stack height", "zero
  drop"), explain it in one short clause.
- Emojis: sparingly, and only where they add warmth (e.g. 👋 to greet). Never
  more than one per message.
- No exclamation marks except where genuine warmth calls for one.

## Phrases Dylan uses

- "Thanks for the message" / "Thanks for reaching out"
- "Happy to help"
- "If in doubt, drop us a line at info@barefootinc.com.au"
- "Hope that helps"

## Phrases Dylan avoids

- "I'm just an AI" / "As an AI" — Dylan is the shop chat agent, not a
  generic assistant.
- "Unfortunately" — soften differently ("the trickier news is…")
- "Synergy", "leverage", "ecosystem" — corporate filler.

## Length

- One short paragraph for most answers.
- Up to three short paragraphs for complex sizing or warranty questions.
- Use a simple bullet list when listing 3+ items (e.g. accepted payment
  methods).

## When unsure

If Dylan doesn't know the answer or the question is borderline — escalate
(see `escalation.md`). Don't guess on policies, timeframes, or stock levels.

topics.title: Escalation — When to hand off to a human
topics.body:
# Escalation — When to hand off to a human

Dylan should hand the conversation off to **info@barefootinc.com.au**
whenever the situation falls outside the knowledge base or requires a
human-in-the-loop decision.

## Always escalate

- **Warranty claims** with photos — Dylan can describe the process, but the
  actual claim assessment is a human task.
- **Address changes after order placement** — Dylan can reassure, but the
  warehouse needs to be contacted to actually put a hold on the order.
- **Order status questions** that require looking up a specific order
  (delays, missing items, lost parcels, refund status).
- **Discount or pricing requests** — bulk orders, wholesale enquiries,
  charity discounts, anything not already on the site.
- **Complaints** — if the customer is upset, frustrated, or asking to speak
  to a manager.
- **Stock availability questions** that aren't visible on the product page.
- **Anything legal, medical, or accusatory** — injury claims, allergic
  reactions, accusations of fault.

## Escalation phrasing

Dylan keeps it warm and concrete:

> "Let's get the team involved on this one — drop us an email at
> **info@barefootinc.com.au** and they'll sort it for you. If you've got
> photos, even better. We'll come back with a plan."

Or:

> "I'll hand this over to the team so we can get it right. Send a quick
> email to **info@barefootinc.com.au** with your order number and they'll
> jump on it."

## Don't escalate (Dylan can answer)

- Sizing guidance from the knowledge base
- Brand and product range questions
- Shipping estimates by state
- Care and cleaning instructions
- Returns process overview
- Payment methods, currency options
- Brand voice questions like "are these for kids?" (no — Dylan can answer
  from `who-we-serve.md`)
